CLUB PICNIC.


The members of the Winters
club are planning picnic to bt
given on July 4. The place has mo-
yet been decided upon. Mrs. J. M
Walker has charge of the arrange
ments and the club members are
looking forward to a most enjoy
able time.

PICNIC AT MANITO.
The Joan Of Arc court No. 459
Women's Catholic Order of Forest
ers, will give picnic on the after
noon of June % at Mantle park.
MINNESOTA CLUB TO ENJOY


OUTING.


An important event among the
series of affairs given by the mem
bers of the Minnesota club will be
picnic which will be given at Man
ito park, July IL.


John H. Bowers and A. G. Gray
have the arrangements in charge
and 1t is expected the open air pic
mic will be one of the largest and
most enjoyable events of the sea
son.


RECITAL TONIGHT.
Miss Lily Courtney Snow will
present her pupils in recital this
evening in her studio at Courtney
hall. This will be the last recital
of the seasons series.


& & &
PICNIC FOR IA CicEA CLUE
The La Cicea club will enjoy a
picnic on June rs. They are plan
ming to journey down the Spokane
river.

OFFICERS TO BE INSTALLED
The Freya chapter No. 1 in the
Scandinavian Sisterhood of Amer
lea will hold a meeting for the in
stallation of officers July S. Mrs
A. G. Clausen, recently elected pres
ident, will preside.

ENGAGEMENT ANNOUNCED.
There is a ripple of pleasant sur
prise among society and the mus
ical people concerning the an
nouncement Of the engagement OF
Mr.. Carlos Huntington, prominent
young lawyer, and Miss Margaret
w. Kissinger of Williamsport, Pa.
Mr.. Huntington Is a graduate oil
the University of Minnesota and
since his arrival in this city has
been prominent in business and
musical circles.

TO WE'D NEXT MONTH.
Cards are out announcing the en-
gagement of Miss Edith Archer and
Mr.. Arie Hover. The wedding will
be celebrated July 2. 1t will be a
quiet affair, only the immediate
relatives being invited.

MEETING POSTPONED.
The meeting of the Pocahontas
lodge which was to have been held
tonight has been postponed until
the first Saturday of next month.


& z Z


LUNCHEON FOR CLUB.
The members of the Sunshine
club of the First Baptist church
were guests at the home of Mrs.
A. M. Cole yesterday afternoon.
A delightful luncheon was served,
covers being placed for rs guests.
Following the luncheon and bust
ness session Mrs. M. J. Reynolds
gave an interesting talk on woman
suffrage.
